comment: v0.3 vote ablation. Generic encoder + question-aware NLI (same Guard as results_ragtruth_q_v1.json), only min_entail_votes varied. HaluEval QA exposes a degenerate edge case: each test case has a single 1-chunk corpus (the knowledge snippet IS the corpus). Therefore the maximum achievable entail_votes per claim is 1. With min_votes >= 2 every claim becomes HALLUCINATION_FLAG — the Guard collapses to a flag-everything classifier. The apparent F1 lift (0.639 -> 0.667) is the trivial all-positive baseline on a 50/50-balanced set, NOT a genuine precision improvement from multi-evidence agreement. Re-test on RAGTruth-shaped multi-chunk corpora before drawing conclusions about the policy.

ablation.behaviour: normal — votes=1 is legacy max-only policy
DEGENERATE — flag-everything; max achievable votes per case is 1 because each case has a 1-chunk corpus

interpretation: The vote-policy lever is structurally unmeasurable on HaluEval QA. The ablation must be re-run on a benchmark with multi-chunk per-case corpora (e.g. RAGTruth, when public mirror returns) for a meaningful read on whether min_votes >= 2 trades recall for precision.
